+0.5% — Monday relief rally markup on the LAB sleeve (INTC/ARKK/NVDA); $75 cash->CORE is an internal reallocation, not a deposit, so it does not break the chain

growth_chart.projection_basis: Possible future value one year out: today's $149.22 account value compounded at the CLU 1.25%/mo target -> ~$173 by 2027-07, performance-only with no new deposits. Confidence stays low — only ~1.5 weeks of live history, a $100 mid-week deposit dominated week-1 value, and the signal engine has been stale/SAFE-MODE for over a day so recent sessions took no signal-based action. The TWR return_index sits at 0.969 (~-3.1% net of deposits since inception). The site recomputes this 12-month trajectory from the latest account value x the CLU monthly target each Mon/Wed/Fri close as deposit-free history accumulates. Layer contributions on top with the Added-Funds Sim.

The 3:20 PM run missed again, and SAFE MODE has recurred across every session since — no signal-based action has been possible for over a day. Engine cron/runner reliability is the binding constraint on the book, not signal quality.

lab.note: No signal-conditioned LAB trades graded yet (1 pre-engine baseline MRVL -1.0; INTC matured 07-01, ARKK/NVDA mature 07-02 — first vs-QQQ grades land Friday 07-03 at §14.1 grading). LAB 25.8% > 20% staging cap, dilutes via base growth — no force-sell (§17.3). Gate bar stays 3/5 until there is enough graded data to justify tightening.

intelligence_evolution.summary: Established the 3-tier framework and first positions across semis + innovation ETF, built the CORE tier 0%->50% by routing deferred idle cash (VOO/QQQ/SOXX), then opened the DRIP tier via a reconciled JEPQ seed (0%->24%) for a near-target 26/50/24 book. Completed the first full stop-loss cycle (MRVL -5.71%) and recalibrated LAB stops to ATR-based sizing. The live signal engine (34 tickers) came online but proved intermittent — it has now missed several consecutive scheduled runs, leaving the matrix >24h stale and forcing back-to-back SAFE-MODE sessions.
intelligence_evolution.key_insight: The tier structure held up under a -2.5% semi-selloff day (INTC -9%, SOXX -6.5%, MU -10.5%) — broad ETFs and the new DRIP leg damped the single-sector shock. Three systems validated under fire: the stop-loss loop (MRVL), the compound gate catching an overbought false-promote (PANW), and the §2.5 staleness guard halting trading on a stale matrix. The dominant open risk is now operational (engine cron reliability), not strategic — it is the only thing blocking signal-based action.
intelligence_evolution.next_phase: Phase 2 — Pattern Recognition (target: fix engine reliability; first signal-conditioned LAB grades vs QQQ Friday 07-03; build DRIP to 30%)

strategy_dna.note: Hard guardrail. Never evaluated, never overridden.
Enforced pre-trade via get_portfolio. No exceptions.
LAB entries require the earnings block + DO_NOT_TRADE check, then >=3/5 quant criteria. Reversed PANW's thesis-only promotion.

Recurring — the engine has missed several consecutive runs, leaving the matrix >24h stale. No new entries on stale signals; existing positions managed only. Working as designed, but the engine misses are the binding constraint.

strategy_dna.rule: Tiered Stops (LAB ATR-based / CORE + DRIP none)
strategy_dna.note: LAB fractional positions use manual/agentic market-sell checks each session at their ATR stop_pct (INTC 12.6 / ARKK 5.2 / NVDA 5.0). CORE/DRIP carry no stop (§13/§15.4) — SOXX -6.5% today held, not stopped.
New/idle capital routes to the most underweight tier vs stage targets (DRIP > ETF > Stock tiebreak). $75 idle cash -> CORE (0%->50%); $36 -> DRIP (0%->24%). DRIP (-6.3pp) is still next.
Deposit-free return chained each Mon/Wed/Fri close. Internal reallocations (cash->ETF) excluded. Index at 0.969 after a -0.6% Mon->Wed (Tue +1.9% / Wed -2.5%).
New entries at the 6:00 AM and 10:45 AM windows only. After-Hours is analysis/planning — no new entries.
MRVL->SOXX (done, stop-out), ARKK->QQQ (+10%, cur +5.4%), INTC->SOXX (+15%, cur ~flat after -9% today). Shifting the book toward an ETF-anchored model to cut single-name noise.

risk_profile.notes: Now a 3-tier, 7-position book: LAB (3 single names) + CORE (3 broad/sector ETFs) + DRIP (JEPQ) — the DRIP leg opened this week improves diversification (score 5 -> 6). Cash buffer near zero (0.25%, $0.37) — fully deployed. The live concentration is semis: SOXX + INTC + NVDA ~ $49.8 (~33% of the account), which drove today's -2.45% on a semi-only selloff. SOXX diversifies single-name risk but not sector risk; growing DRIP toward 30% is the next concrete diversification step.

weekly_recap.summary: Week 2 through Wednesday. The week opened by deploying the $111 dry powder: $75 to CORE (VOO/QQQ/SOXX $25 ea, 0%->50%) and a reconciled $36 JEPQ buy opening DRIP (0%->24%), leaving a near-target 26/50/24 book. Tuesday marked the LAB sleeve up on the quarter-end rally; Wednesday reversed it with a sharp semi selloff (INTC -9%, SOXX -6.5%, MU -10.5%), netting the account roughly flat for the week (+0.27%) with ARKK (+5.4% from entry) the standout. The signal engine went stale for over a day (missed the 3:20 PM run again), forcing consecutive SAFE-MODE sessions and no signal-based action. MU fell into its $1,050-1,100 entry zone but stays deferred. First signal-conditioned LAB grades vs QQQ land Friday 07-03.
